hypothesis: If, in semi-supervised learning on molecular graphs, we train an ensemble of models with a γ-weighted consistency loss to the gradient-detached ensemble mean on unlabeled data, then test error will decrease and calibration will improve relative to supervised, mean-teacher, and pseudo-labeling baselines across architectures and datasets—and the gains will increase with ensemble size—because ensemble consensus provides low-variance soft targets that regularize against overfitting to noisy pseudo-labels.
